# Interactive Map

A full-featured interactive map module built on Leaflet.js with OpenStreetMap tiles.

## Features

- **Map View**: Pan and zoom an OpenStreetMap-based tile layer.
- **Markers**: Add markers with a title, description, and category. Each marker displays a popup on click.
- **Circles**: Draw circles on the map to highlight geographic areas with a configurable radius and color.
- **Location Search**: Search for addresses or places using the Nominatim geocoding API. Results are centered on the map.
- **Sidebar**: A scrollable sidebar lists every marker. Clicking a sidebar entry centers the map on that marker and opens its popup.
- **Categories**: Markers are assigned to color-coded categories. The sidebar supports filtering by category.

## API

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/` | Render the full map page |
| GET | `/api/markers` | List all markers (optional `?category=` filter) |
| POST | `/api/markers` | Create a marker `{ title, description, lat, lng, category }` |
| DELETE | `/api/markers/:id` | Remove a marker |
| GET | `/api/circles` | List all circles |
| POST | `/api/circles` | Create a circle `{ lat, lng, radius, color, label }` |
| DELETE | `/api/circles/:id` | Remove a circle |
| GET | `/api/categories` | List available categories |
| GET | `/api/search?q=` | Geocode an address via Nominatim |

## Usage

```ts
import { serve } from "./index";
serve({ port: 3000 });
```

[
  { "id": "default", "label": "Default", "color": "#3388ff" },
  { "id": "food", "label": "Food & Drink", "color": "#e74c3c" },
  { "id": "nature", "label": "Nature", "color": "#27ae60" },
  { "id": "culture", "label": "Culture", "color": "#8e44ad" },
  { "id": "shopping", "label": "Shopping", "color": "#f39c12" },
  { "id": "transport", "label": "Transport", "color": "#2c3e50" }
]

# Interactive Map

An interactive map module powered by Leaflet.js and OpenStreetMap tiles.

## Features

- **Map View**: Pan and zoom an OpenStreetMap-based tile layer.
- **Markers**: Add markers with a title, description, category, and custom color. Click any marker to view its popup.
- **Circles**: Draw circles on the map to highlight areas with a center, radius, and color.
- **Location Search**: Search for a place name using the Nominatim geocoder and fly to it on the map.
- **Sidebar**: Lists all markers grouped by category. Click a marker in the sidebar to center the map on it.
- **Category Filtering**: Color-coded pins per category. Toggle categories on/off in the sidebar to show or hide markers.

## API

### `GET /`
Returns the full interactive map HTML page.

### `GET /api/markers`
Returns all markers as JSON. Supports `?category=` query param to filter.

### `POST /api/markers`
Create a marker. Body: `{ "title", "description", "lat", "lng", "category" }`.

### `DELETE /api/markers/:id`
Remove a marker by ID.

### `GET /api/circles`
Returns all circles as JSON.

### `POST /api/circles`
Create a circle. Body: `{ "title", "lat", "lng", "radius", "color" }`.

### `DELETE /api/circles/:id`
Remove a circle by ID.

### `GET /api/search?q=<query>`
Geocode a place name via Nominatim and return coordinates.

}

export type MapMarker = {
  id: string
  title: string
  description: string
  lat: number
  lng: number
  category: MarkerCategory
}

export type MapCircle = {
  id: string
  title: string
  lat: number
  lng: number
  radius: number
  color: string
}

export type SearchResult = {
  lat: number
  lng: number
  displayName: string
}

# Calendar Module

A scheduling and calendar module that displays a monthly calendar grid with event indicators, supports full event management, and color-codes events by category.

## Features

- **Monthly Grid View**: Renders a full month calendar with day cells showing event indicators
- **Day View**: Lists all events for a selected date with full details
- **Event Management**: Create, read, update, and delete events
- **Event Properties**: Title, date, start/end time, description, and category
- **Recurrence**: Support for daily, weekly, and monthly recurring events
- **Category Color-Coding**: Events are color-coded by category (work, personal, health, social)
- **Filtering**: Filter events by category or date range

## Usage

### View monthly calendar
Render a monthly grid for a given year and month showing days with event indicators.

### View day events
Select a date to see all events scheduled for that day, sorted by start time.

### Create event
Provide title, date, start time, end time, description, category, and optional recurrence rule.

### Edit event
Update any event field by event ID.

### Delete event
Remove an event by ID.

## Categories
- **work** — Blue indicators for work-related events
- **personal** — Pink indicators for personal events
- **health** — Green indicators for health & fitness events
- **social** — Amber indicators for social gatherings

export type {
  CalendarEvent,
  EventCategory,
  RecurrenceRule,
  DayCell,
  MonthGrid,
} from './types'

export { CATEGORY_COLORS } from './types'

export {
  getAllEvents,
  getEventById,
  getEventsForDate,
  getEventsForMonth,
  createEvent,
  updateEvent,
  deleteEvent,
  getEventsByCategory,
} from './events'

export {
  buildMonthGrid,
  getNextMonth,
  getPrevMonth,
  DAY_NAMES,
  MONTH_NAMES,
} from './calendar'

export {
  renderMonthGrid,
  renderDayView,
  renderEventCard,
  renderEventForm,
  renderCategoryLegend,
  renderEventIndicator,
} from './render'

description: Monthly calendar with event scheduling, recurrence, day view, and category color-coding

  contentType: events

---

# Calendar Module

A scheduling and calendar module that displays a monthly grid with event indicators and supports full event lifecycle management.

## Features

- **Monthly Grid View** — Renders a full month calendar with day cells showing event indicator dots color-coded by category.
- **Day View** — Select any date to see a chronological list of all events for that day, with full details.
- **Event CRUD** — Create, read, update, and delete events with title, date, start/end time, description, category, and recurrence.
- **Recurrence** — Events can recur daily, weekly, or monthly. Recurring instances are expanded at query time.
- **Categories** — Four built-in categories with distinct color coding:
  - `work` (blue)
  - `personal` (pink)
  - `health` (green)
  - `social` (amber)

## Usage

### View month

```
GET /calendar?year=2026&month=3
```

### View day

```
GET /calendar/day?date=2026-03-15
```

### Create event

name: calendar
description: Monthly calendar with event scheduling, day views, recurrence, and category color-coding
metadata:
  contentType: scheduling
  structure: collection
  boundary: all

---

# Calendar Module

A scheduling and calendar module that displays a monthly calendar grid with event indicators and supports full event lifecycle management.

## Features

- **Monthly Grid View**: Renders a full month calendar with day cells showing event indicator dots color-coded by category.
- **Day View**: Lists all events for a selected date with times, titles, descriptions, and category badges.
- **Event Management**: Create, edit, and delete events with title, date, start time, end time, description, and category.
- **Recurrence**: Events support optional recurrence patterns — daily, weekly, or monthly — with automatic expansion into the viewed date range.
- **Categories**: Events are classified as `work`, `personal`, `health`, or `social`, each rendered with a distinct color.

## Usage

### Viewing the calendar

Request the monthly view for any year/month. The grid shows Sunday–Saturday columns with colored dots on days that have events.

### Creating an event

Provide title, date (YYYY-MM-DD), startTime (HH:MM), endTime (HH:MM), description, category, and optionally a recurrence of `daily`, `weekly`, or `monthly`.

### Editing / deleting

Reference an event by its ID to update fields or remove it entirely.

## Data

Events are persisted in `data/events.json` as a JSON array.

---

# Chart Generator

Generates SVG bar charts, line charts, and pie charts from JSON array data.

## Features

- **Auto-detection**: Automatically identifies numeric vs categorical fields in your data
- **Chart types**: Bar, Line, and Pie charts
- **Customization**: Chart title, axis labels, and per-series color overrides
- **SVG export**: Download any generated chart as an SVG file
- **JSON editor**: Built-in editor with syntax highlighting for data input

## Usage

1. Paste or type JSON data into the editor. Data should be an array of objects:
   ```json
   [
     { "month": "Jan", "sales": 120, "expenses": 80 },
     { "month": "Feb", "sales": 150, "expenses": 95 }
   ]
   ```
2. Select a chart type (bar, line, or pie).
3. Choose which field to use as the category axis and which numeric fields to plot.
4. Optionally set a title, axis labels, and custom colors.
5. Click "Generate" to render the chart.
6. Click "Download SVG" to save the chart.

## API

### `POST /api/chart`

Generate a chart from JSON data.

**Body:**
```json
{
  "data": [{ "label": "A", "value": 10 }],
  "chartType": "bar",
  "categoryField": "label",
  "valueFields": ["value"],
  "title": "My Chart",
  "xLabel": "Category",
  "yLabel": "Value",
  "colors": ["#4e79a7", "#f28e2b"]
}
```

**Response:** SVG string

export type ChartType = 'bar' | 'line' | 'pie'

export type FieldInfo = {
  name: string
  type: 'numeric' | 'categorical'
}

export type ChartConfig = {
  data: Record<string, unknown>[]
  chartType: ChartType
  categoryField: string
  valueFields: string[]
  title?: string
  xLabel?: string
  yLabel?: string
  colors?: string[]
  width?: number
  height?: number
}

export type DetectedSchema = {
  fields: FieldInfo[]
  numericFields: string[]
  categoricalFields: string[]
}

export const DEFAULT_COLORS = [
  '#4e79a7',
  '#f28e2b',
  '#e15759',
  '#76b7b2',
  '#59a14f',
  '#edc948',
  '#b07aa1',
  '#ff9da7',
  '#9c755f',
  '#bab0ac',
]

export const CHART_DEFAULTS = {
  width: 640,
  height: 400,
  padding: { top: 50, right: 30, bottom: 60, left: 70 },
} as const

# Chart Generator

Generate publication-ready charts from JSON data arrays.

## Features

- **Chart types**: Bar, Line, Pie
- **Auto field detection**: Automatically classifies fields as numeric or categorical
- **Customization**: Chart title, axis labels, color palettes
- **SVG export**: Download any chart as an SVG file
- **JSON editor**: Built-in syntax-highlighted editor for data input

## Usage

1. Paste or type a JSON array of objects into the editor
2. The module auto-detects numeric and categorical fields
3. Select a chart type (bar, line, or pie)
4. Choose which fields to plot on each axis
5. Customize title, labels, and colors
6. Download the rendered chart as SVG

## Data Format

Input must be a JSON array of objects with consistent keys:

```json
[
  { "category": "A", "value": 10, "count": 5 },
  { "category": "B", "value": 20, "count": 8 },
  { "category": "C", "value": 15, "count": 3 }
]
```

Numeric fields are auto-detected for value axes. String fields are used as category labels.

[
  { "month": "Jan", "revenue": 4200, "expenses": 3100 },
  { "month": "Feb", "revenue": 5100, "expenses": 3400 },
  { "month": "Mar", "revenue": 4800, "expenses": 3200 },
  { "month": "Apr", "revenue": 6200, "expenses": 3800 },
  { "month": "May", "revenue": 7100, "expenses": 4100 },
  { "month": "Jun", "revenue": 6800, "expenses": 4500 }
]

# Prompt Chart Generator

Generates SVG charts from JSON data arrays. Supports bar charts, line charts, and pie charts.

## Features

- **Auto-detect fields**: Automatically classifies fields as numeric or categorical
- **Chart types**: Bar, line, and pie charts
- **Customization**: Chart title, axis labels, and color palette
- **SVG download**: Export any chart as an SVG file
- **JSON editor**: Syntax-highlighted editor for data input

## Usage

1. Paste or type JSON data into the editor (array of objects with numeric and string fields)
2. Select a chart type
3. Choose which fields to use for categories and values (or let auto-detect handle it)
4. Customize title, labels, and colors
5. Download the rendered chart as SVG

## Data Format

```json
[
  { "category": "A", "value": 10 },
  { "category": "B", "value": 25 },
  { "category": "C", "value": 15 }
]
```

Fields with all-numeric values are detected as numeric; others are treated as categorical.

export type ChartType = 'bar' | 'line' | 'pie'

export type FieldType = 'numeric' | 'categorical'

export type FieldInfo = {
  name: string
  type: FieldType
}

export type ChartConfig = {
  type: ChartType
  title?: string
  xLabel?: string
  yLabel?: string
  colors?: string[]
  categoryField?: string
  valueField?: string
  width?: number
  height?: number
}

export type DataRecord = Record<string, string | number>

export type DetectedFields = {
  numeric: string[]
  categorical: string[]
}

export const DEFAULT_COLORS = [

import { h } from 'plaited'
import type { ChartConfig, DataRecord } from '../types'
import { DEFAULT_COLORS, DEFAULT_WIDTH, DEFAULT_HEIGHT } from '../types'
import { PADDING, extractValues, extractLabels, niceMax, generateTicks, escapeHtml } from './utils'

export const renderBarChart = (data: DataRecord[], config: ChartConfig): string => {
  const {
    title = '',
    xLabel = '',
    yLabel = '',
    colors = DEFAULT_COLORS,
    categoryField = '',
    valueField = '',
    width = DEFAULT_WIDTH,
    height = DEFAULT_HEIGHT,
  } = config

  const labels = extractLabels(data, categoryField)
  const values = extractValues(data, valueField)
  const max = niceMax(values)
  const ticks = generateTicks(max)

  const chartW = width - PADDING.left - PADDING.right
  const chartH = height - PADDING.top - PADDING.bottom
  const barWidth = Math.max(1, (chartW / labels.length) * 0.7)
  const barGap = (chartW / labels.length) * 0.3

  const bars = values.map((v, i) => {
    const barH = (v / max) * chartH
    const x = PADDING.left + i * (barWidth + barGap) + barGap / 2
    const y = PADDING.top + chartH - barH
    const color = colors[i % colors.length]

    return h('g', {},
      h('rect', {
        x: String(x),
        y: String(y),
        width: String(barWidth),
        height: String(barH),
        fill: color,
        rx: '3',
      }),
      h('text', {
        x: String(x + barWidth / 2),
        y: String(y - 6),
        'text-anchor': 'middle',
        'font-size': '12',
        'font-family': 'system-ui, sans-serif',
        fill: '#333',
      }, String(v)),
      h('text', {
        x: String(x + barWidth / 2),
        y: String(PADDING.top + chartH + 20),
        'text-anchor': 'middle',
        'font-size': '12',
        'font-family': 'system-ui, sans-serif',
        fill: '#555',
      }, escapeHtml(labels[i])),
    )
  })

  const gridLines = ticks.map((tick) => {
    const y = PADDING.top + chartH - (tick / max) * chartH
    return h('g', {},
      h('line', {
        x1: String(PADDING.left),
        y1: String(y),
        x2: String(width - PADDING.right),
        y2: String(y),
        stroke: '#e0e0e0',
        'stroke-width': '1',
      }),
      h('text', {
        x: String(PADDING.left - 10),
        y: String(y + 4),
        'text-anchor': 'end',
        'font-size': '11',
        'font-family': 'system-ui, sans-serif',
        fill: '#777',
      }, String(tick)),
    )
  })

  const titleEl = title
    ? h('text', {
        x: String(width / 2),
        y: '30',
        'text-anchor': 'middle',
        'font-size': '18',
        'font-weight': 'bold',
        'font-family': 'system-ui, sans-serif',
        fill: '#222',
      }, escapeHtml(title))
    : ''

  const xLabelEl = xLabel
    ? h('text', {
        x: String(width / 2),
        y: String(height - 10),
        'text-anchor': 'middle',
        'font-size': '13',
        'font-family': 'system-ui, sans-serif',
        fill: '#555',
      }, escapeHtml(xLabel))
    : ''

  const yLabelEl = yLabel
    ? h('text', {
        x: '15',
        y: String(PADDING.top + chartH / 2),
        'text-anchor': 'middle',
        'font-size': '13',
        'font-family': 'system-ui, sans-serif',
        fill: '#555',
        transform: `rotate(-90, 15, ${PADDING.top + chartH / 2})`,
      }, escapeHtml(yLabel))
    : ''

  const axes = h('g', {},
    h('line', {
      x1: String(PADDING.left),
      y1: String(PADDING.top),
      x2: String(PADDING.left),
      y2: String(PADDING.top + chartH),
      stroke: '#999',
      'stroke-width': '1.5',
    }),
    h('line', {
      x1: String(PADDING.left),
      y1: String(PADDING.top + chartH),
      x2: String(width - PADDING.right),
      y2: String(PADDING.top + chartH),
      stroke: '#999',
      'stroke-width': '1.5',
    }),
  )

  return h('svg', {
    xmlns: 'http://www.w3.org/2000/svg',
    width: String(width),
    height: String(height),
    viewBox: `0 0 ${width} ${height}`,
  },
    h('rect', { width: String(width), height: String(height), fill: '#fafafa' }),
    titleEl,
    ...gridLines,
    axes,
    ...bars,
    xLabelEl,
    yLabelEl,
  )
}

import { h } from 'plaited'
import type { ChartConfig, DataRecord } from '../types'
import { DEFAULT_COLORS, DEFAULT_WIDTH, DEFAULT_HEIGHT } from '../types'
import { PADDING, extractValues, extractLabels, niceMax, generateTicks, escapeHtml } from './utils'

export const renderLineChart = (data: DataRecord[], config: ChartConfig): string => {
  const {
    title = '',
    xLabel = '',
    yLabel = '',
    colors = DEFAULT_COLORS,
    categoryField = '',
    valueField = '',
    width = DEFAULT_WIDTH,
    height = DEFAULT_HEIGHT,
  } = config

  const labels = extractLabels(data, categoryField)
  const values = extractValues(data, valueField)
  const max = niceMax(values)
  const ticks = generateTicks(max)

  const chartW = width - PADDING.left - PADDING.right
  const chartH = height - PADDING.top - PADDING.bottom

  const points = values.map((v, i) => {
    const x = PADDING.left + (i / Math.max(1, labels.length - 1)) * chartW
    const y = PADDING.top + chartH - (v / max) * chartH
    return { x, y, v }
  })

  const polylinePoints = points.map((p) => `${p.x},${p.y}`).join(' ')
  const color = colors[0]

  const areaPath = [
    `M ${points[0].x},${PADDING.top + chartH}`,
    `L ${points[0].x},${points[0].y}`,
    ...points.slice(1).map((p) => `L ${p.x},${p.y}`),
    `L ${points[points.length - 1].x},${PADDING.top + chartH}`,
    'Z',
  ].join(' ')

  const dots = points.map((p, i) =>
    h('g', {},
      h('circle', {
        cx: String(p.x),
        cy: String(p.y),
        r: '5',
        fill: color,
        stroke: '#fff',
        'stroke-width': '2',
      }),
      h('text', {
        x: String(p.x),
        y: String(p.y - 12),
        'text-anchor': 'middle',
        'font-size': '11',
        'font-family': 'system-ui, sans-serif',
        fill: '#333',
      }, String(p.v)),
      h('text', {
        x: String(p.x),
        y: String(PADDING.top + chartH + 20),
        'text-anchor': 'middle',
        'font-size': '12',
        'font-family': 'system-ui, sans-serif',
        fill: '#555',
      }, escapeHtml(labels[i])),
    ),
  )

  const gridLines = ticks.map((tick) => {
    const y = PADDING.top + chartH - (tick / max) * chartH
    return h('g', {},
      h('line', {
        x1: String(PADDING.left),
        y1: String(y),
        x2: String(width - PADDING.right),
        y2: String(y),
        stroke: '#e0e0e0',
        'stroke-width': '1',
      }),
      h('text', {
        x: String(PADDING.left - 10),
        y: String(y + 4),
        'text-anchor': 'end',
        'font-size': '11',
        'font-family': 'system-ui, sans-serif',
        fill: '#777',
      }, String(tick)),
    )
  })

  const titleEl = title
    ? h('text', {
        x: String(width / 2),
        y: '30',
        'text-anchor': 'middle',
        'font-size': '18',
        'font-weight': 'bold',
        'font-family': 'system-ui, sans-serif',
        fill: '#222',
      }, escapeHtml(title))
    : ''

  const xLabelEl = xLabel
    ? h('text', {
        x: String(width / 2),
        y: String(height - 10),
        'text-anchor': 'middle',
        'font-size': '13',
        'font-family': 'system-ui, sans-serif',
        fill: '#555',
      }, escapeHtml(xLabel))
    : ''

  const yLabelEl = yLabel
    ? h('text', {
        x: '15',
        y: String(PADDING.top + chartH / 2),
        'text-anchor': 'middle',
        'font-size': '13',
        'font-family': 'system-ui, sans-serif',
        fill: '#555',
        transform: `rotate(-90, 15, ${PADDING.top + chartH / 2})`,
      }, escapeHtml(yLabel))
    : ''

  const axes = h('g', {},
    h('line', {
      x1: String(PADDING.left),
      y1: String(PADDING.top),
      x2: String(PADDING.left),
      y2: String(PADDING.top + chartH),
      stroke: '#999',
      'stroke-width': '1.5',
    }),
    h('line', {
      x1: String(PADDING.left),
      y1: String(PADDING.top + chartH),
      x2: String(width - PADDING.right),
      y2: String(PADDING.top + chartH),
      stroke: '#999',
      'stroke-width': '1.5',
    }),
  )

  return h('svg', {
    xmlns: 'http://www.w3.org/2000/svg',
    width: String(width),
    height: String(height),
    viewBox: `0 0 ${width} ${height}`,
  },
    h('rect', { width: String(width), height: String(height), fill: '#fafafa' }),
    titleEl,
    ...gridLines,
    axes,
    h('path', {
      d: areaPath,
      fill: color,
      'fill-opacity': '0.1',
    }),
    h('polyline', {
      points: polylinePoints,
      fill: 'none',
      stroke: color,
      'stroke-width': '2.5',
      'stroke-linejoin': 'round',
    }),
    ...dots,
    xLabelEl,
    yLabelEl,
  )
}

---
name: color-palette
description: Generate harmonious color palettes using color theory with contrast checking
metadata:
  contentType: design
  structure: object
  boundary: all
  scale: "2"
---

# Color Palette Generator

Generates harmonious color palettes from a base color using established color theory rules.

## Supported Harmony Types

- **Complementary** — two colors opposite on the color wheel (180° apart)
- **Analogous** — three colors adjacent on the color wheel (±30°)
- **Triadic** — three colors evenly spaced (120° apart)
- **Split-complementary** — base color + two colors adjacent to its complement (±150°)
- **Tetradic** — four colors forming a rectangle on the color wheel (60°/180°/240°)

## Features

- Accept base color via hex string or color picker
- Display swatches with hex, RGB, and HSL values
- Copy any color value to clipboard
- WCAG AA/AAA contrast ratio checker between any two palette colors

## Usage

Provide a hex color (e.g. `#3a86ff`) and a harmony type. The module returns a palette of colors with full value representations and renders interactive swatches with contrast compliance badges.

## Harmony Modes

- **Complementary** — Two colors opposite on the color wheel (180° apart)
- **Analogous** — Three colors adjacent on the wheel (±30°)
- **Triadic** — Three colors evenly spaced (120° apart)
- **Split-Complementary** — Base color plus two colors adjacent to its complement (±150°)
- **Tetradic** — Four colors forming a rectangle on the wheel (60°/120° pairs)

## Usage

1. Provide a base color as a hex value (e.g. `#3a86ff`) or use the color picker
2. Select a harmony mode
3. View generated swatches with hex, RGB, and HSL values
4. Click any color value to copy it to the clipboard
5. Select two colors to check WCAG AA/AAA contrast compliance

## Contrast Checker

The built-in contrast checker evaluates color pairs against WCAG 2.1 guidelines:
- **AA Normal Text** — ratio ≥ 4.5:1
- **AA Large Text** — ratio ≥ 3:1
- **AAA Normal Text** — ratio ≥ 7:1
- **AAA Large Text** — ratio ≥ 4.5:1

import { type Color, colorFromHsl, normalizeHue } from './color.ts'

export type HarmonyMode =
  | 'complementary'
  | 'analogous'
  | 'triadic'
  | 'split-complementary'
  | 'tetradic'

export type Palette = {
  mode: HarmonyMode
  base: Color
  colors: Color[]
}

const shiftHue = (base: Color, degrees: number): Color =>
  colorFromHsl({
    h: normalizeHue(base.hsl.h + degrees),
    s: base.hsl.s,
    l: base.hsl.l,
  })

export const generateComplementary = (base: Color): Color[] => [base, shiftHue(base, 180)]

export const generateAnalogous = (base: Color): Color[] => [
  shiftHue(base, -30),
  base,
  shiftHue(base, 30),
]

export const generateTriadic = (base: Color): Color[] => [
  base,
  shiftHue(base, 120),
  shiftHue(base, 240),
]

export const generateSplitComplementary = (base: Color): Color[] => [
  base,
  shiftHue(base, 150),
  shiftHue(base, 210),
]

export const generateTetradic = (base: Color): Color[] => [
  base,
  shiftHue(base, 60),
  shiftHue(base, 180),
  shiftHue(base, 240),
]

const generators: Record<HarmonyMode, (base: Color) => Color[]> = {
  complementary: generateComplementary,
  analogous: generateAnalogous,
  triadic: generateTriadic,
  'split-complementary': generateSplitComplementary,
  tetradic: generateTetradic,
}

export const generatePalette = (base: Color, mode: HarmonyMode): Palette => ({
  mode,
  base,
  colors: generators[mode](base),
})

export const harmonyModes: HarmonyMode[] = [
  'complementary',
  'analogous',
  'triadic',
  'split-complementary',
  'tetradic',
]

# Color Palette Generator

Generates harmonious color palettes from a base color using established color theory rules.

## Supported Harmony Types

- **Complementary** — two colors opposite on the color wheel (180deg apart)
- **Analogous** — three colors adjacent on the color wheel (30deg apart)
- **Triadic** — three colors evenly spaced (120deg apart)

4. Click any color value to copy it to clipboard
5. Select two colors to check WCAG AA/AAA contrast compliance

## Contrast Checker

The built-in contrast checker calculates the luminance ratio between any two selected palette colors and reports compliance with:

- **WCAG AA** — ratio >= 4.5:1 for normal text, >= 3:1 for large text
- **WCAG AAA** — ratio >= 7:1 for normal text, >= 4.5:1 for large text

# Markdown Editor

A full-featured markdown editor with live preview, formatting toolbar, and document management.

## Features

- **Split-pane view**: Editor on the left, rendered preview on the right
- **Live preview**: Preview updates as you type
- **Formatting toolbar**: Buttons for headings, bold, italic, links, images, code blocks, lists, blockquotes, horizontal rules, and tables
- **Document management**: Save documents with a title, load previously saved documents, list all saved documents
- **Standard markdown**: Supports headings, emphasis, links, images, fenced code blocks, ordered/unordered lists, blockquotes, horizontal rules, and tables

## API

- `GET /` — Serves the editor UI
- `GET /api/documents` — List all saved documents
- `GET /api/documents/:id` — Load a specific document
- `POST /api/documents` — Save a new document (body: `{ title, content }`)
- `PUT /api/documents/:id` — Update an existing document
- `DELETE /api/documents/:id` — Delete a document

## Usage

Start the server and open the editor in a browser. Write markdown in the left pane and see the rendered output in the right pane. Use the toolbar buttons to insert formatting. Save and load documents via the document panel.

export type Document = {
  id: string
  title: string
  content: string
  createdAt: string
  updatedAt: string
}

---
name: markdown-editor
description: A split-pane markdown editor with live preview, toolbar, and document save/load
metadata:
  contentType: documents
  structure: form
  boundary: all
  scale: "2"
---

# Markdown Editor

A full-featured markdown editor module with live preview rendering.

## Features

- **Split-pane view**: Editor on the left, rendered HTML preview on the right
- **Toolbar**: Quick-access formatting buttons for headings, bold, italic, links, images, code blocks, lists, blockquotes, horizontal rules, and tables
- **Live preview**: Renders markdown to HTML in real-time as you type
- **Document management**: Save documents with a title, load previously saved documents, and list all saved documents
- **Standard markdown support**: Headings (h1-h6), bold, italic, links, images, fenced code blocks, ordered/unordered lists, blockquotes, horizontal rules, and tables

## Usage

### Endpoints

- `GET /` — Renders the editor UI with split-pane view
- `GET /documents` — Lists all saved documents
- `GET /documents/:id` — Loads a specific document
- `POST /documents` — Saves a new document (body: `{ title, content }`)
- `PUT /documents/:id` — Updates an existing document
- `DELETE /documents/:id` — Deletes a document

### Toolbar Actions

Each toolbar button inserts the corresponding markdown syntax at the cursor position or wraps the selected text.

# Portfolio Gallery

A portfolio gallery module that displays project cards in a responsive grid layout.

## Features

- **Grid View**: Responsive card grid showing thumbnail, title, description, tech tags, and links
- **Filter by Tag**: Click any tech tag to filter projects using that technology
- **Search**: Search projects by title with real-time filtering
- **Sort**: Sort projects by date added (newest/oldest) or alphabetically (A-Z/Z-A)
- **Detail View**: Expand a project to see full description, image carousel with lightbox, and links to live demo and source code

## Data Format

Projects are stored in `data/projects.json` as an array of objects:

```json
{
  "id": "string",
  "title": "string",
  "description": "string",
  "fullDescription": "string",
  "thumbnail": "string (URL)",
  "images": ["string (URL)"],
  "tags": ["string"],
  "demoUrl": "string",
  "sourceUrl": "string",
  "dateAdded": "string (ISO date)"
}
```

- `GET /` — Renders the gallery grid view
- `GET /project/:id` — Renders a single project detail view
- `GET /api/projects` — Returns all projects as JSON
- `GET /api/projects?tag=react&q=dashboard&sort=date-desc` — Filtered/sorted JSON

# Portfolio Gallery

A responsive portfolio gallery module that displays project cards in a grid layout. Each card shows a thumbnail image, title, description, technology tags, and links.

---
name: portfolio-gallery
description: Responsive portfolio gallery with filterable project cards, search, sorting, and lightbox detail views
metadata:
  contentType: projects
  structure: collection
  boundary: all
  scale: "2"
---

# Portfolio Gallery

A module for displaying portfolio projects in a responsive grid layout with rich interaction features.

## Features

- **Project Cards** — Responsive grid of cards showing thumbnail, title, description, tech tags, and links
- **Filter by Tech Tag** — Click any tag to filter projects using that technology
- **Search by Title** — Real-time search to find projects by name
- **Sort** — Order projects by date added (newest/oldest) or alphabetically (A-Z/Z-A)
- **Detail View** — Expanded view with full description, image lightbox carousel, and external links
- **Lightbox Carousel** — Navigate through multiple project images with prev/next controls

## Data Format

Projects are stored as JSONL in `data/projects.jsonl`. Each line is a JSON object:

```json
{
  "id": "unique-id",
  "title": "Project Name",
  "description": "Short description for the card",
  "fullDescription": "Detailed description for the detail view",
  "thumbnail": "/images/thumb.jpg",
  "images": ["/images/img1.jpg", "/images/img2.jpg"],
  "tags": ["TypeScript", "React"],
  "demoUrl": "https://example.com",
  "sourceUrl": "https://github.com/user/repo",
  "dateAdded": "2025-10-15"
}
```

## Routes

- `GET /` — Gallery grid with optional query params: `?tag=`, `?q=`, `?sort=date-desc|date-asc|alpha-asc|alpha-desc`
- `GET /project/:id` — Detail view for a single project

## Usage

```ts
import { createServer } from './index'
const server = createServer({ port: 3000 })
```

export type Project = {
  id: string
  title: string
  description: string
  fullDescription: string
  thumbnail: string
  images: string[]
  tags: string[]
  demoUrl: string
  sourceUrl: string
  dateAdded: string
}

export type SortOption = 'date-desc' | 'date-asc' | 'alpha-asc' | 'alpha-desc'

export type GalleryQuery = {
  tag?: string
  q?: string
  sort?: SortOption
}

It should provide a canvas with freehand drawing, shapes (rectangle, circle, line), a color picker, adjustable brush size, an eraser tool, undo/redo functionality, and the ability to clear the canvas. Support saving drawings as PNG and loading them back. Include a layers panel showing layer thumbnails with visibility toggles.

input.content:
---
name: drawing-canvas
description: Freehand drawing canvas with shapes, layers, undo/redo, and PNG export
metadata:
  contentType: drawing
  structure: object
  boundary: all
  scale: "2"
---

# Drawing Canvas

A full-featured drawing canvas module supporting freehand sketching, geometric shapes, layer management, and image persistence.

## Features

- **Freehand drawing** with adjustable brush size and color
- **Shape tools**: rectangle, circle, and line
- **Eraser tool** for corrections
- **Color picker** with preset palette and custom hex input
- **Brush size** slider (1–50px)
- **Undo / Redo** with full history stack
- **Clear canvas** to start fresh
- **Layers panel** with thumbnails, visibility toggles, add/remove/reorder
- **Save** drawings as PNG to the data directory
- **Load** saved PNG files back onto the canvas

## Usage

```ts
import { createCanvasApp } from './index'

// Mount the drawing app, returns controls for programmatic use
const app = createCanvasApp({ width: 800, height: 600 })

// Save current drawing
await app.save('my-drawing')

// Load a saved drawing
await app.load('my-drawing')

// Programmatic access
app.undo()
app.redo()
app.clear()
app.setTool('rectangle')
app.setColor('#ff0000')
app.setBrushSize(10)
```

## Data Storage

Drawings are saved as PNG files in `data/drawings/`. A manifest at `data/drawings/manifest.json` tracks metadata for each saved drawing.

export { createCanvasApp } from './canvas'
export type { CanvasApp } from './canvas'
export type {
  Tool,
  Point,
  Stroke,
  ShapeStroke,
  DrawAction,
  Layer,
  CanvasState,
  CanvasConfig,
  DrawingManifest,
  DrawingManifestEntry,
} from './types'
export {
  createLayer,
  addActionToLayer,
  removeLastAction,
  toggleLayerVisibility,
  setLayerOpacity,
  renameLayer,
  clearLayer,
  reorderLayers,
} from './layers'
export { pushHistory, undo, redo, canUndo, canRedo } from './history'
export { renderAllLayers, renderStroke, renderLayer, renderLayerThumbnail } from './renderer'
export { saveDrawing, loadDrawingPng, loadDrawingState, listDrawings, deleteDrawing } from './storage'

---
name: drawing-canvas
description: Freehand and shape drawing canvas with layers, undo/redo, and PNG export
metadata:
  contentType: graphic
  structure: object
  boundary: all
  scale: "2"
---

# Drawing Canvas

A browser-based drawing canvas module that supports freehand drawing, geometric shapes, color selection, eraser, and layer management.

## Features

- **Freehand drawing** with adjustable brush size
- **Shape tools**: rectangle, circle, and line
- **Color picker** for stroke and fill colors
- **Eraser tool** that draws with the background color
- **Undo / Redo** history stack
- **Clear canvas** to reset the current layer
- **Layers panel** with thumbnail previews and visibility toggles
- **Save / Load** drawings as PNG files stored in `data/`

## Usage

Start the server and open the canvas in a browser. Select tools from the toolbar, pick colors, adjust brush size, and draw on the canvas. Use the layers panel to manage drawing layers with visibility controls.

### API Endpoints

- `GET /` — serves the drawing canvas UI
- `POST /api/save` — saves current canvas as PNG (base64 body)
- `GET /api/load/:id` — loads a saved PNG drawing
- `GET /api/drawings` — lists saved drawings

# Drawing Canvas

A browser-based drawing canvas module that supports freehand drawing, geometric shapes,
layers, and image export.

## Features

- **Freehand drawing** with adjustable brush size and color
- **Shape tools**: rectangle, circle, and line
- **Eraser tool** that draws with the background color
- **Color picker** for selecting stroke/fill color
- **Brush size** slider for controlling stroke width
- **Undo / Redo** to step backward and forward through history
- **Clear canvas** to reset the current layer
- **Layers panel** with thumbnail previews and visibility toggles
- **Save / Load** drawings as PNG files stored in `data/`

## Usage

Start the server and open the canvas in a browser. Select a tool from the
toolbar, pick a color and brush size, and draw on the canvas. Use the layers
panel on the right to add, remove, reorder, or toggle layer visibility.
Drawings persist as PNG files and can be reloaded later.

metadata:

# Statistics Calculator

Accepts numeric datasets and computes comprehensive descriptive statistics with visual output.

## Input Formats

- **Comma-separated**: `1, 2, 3, 4, 5`
- **JSON array**: `[1, 2, 3, 4, 5]`
- **Named datasets**: provide a name when adding each dataset for side-by-side comparison

## Computed Statistics

| Statistic | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| Mean | Arithmetic average |
| Median | Middle value |
| Mode | Most frequent value(s) |
| Standard Deviation | Spread of data from mean |
| Variance | Square of standard deviation |
| Min / Max | Smallest and largest values |
| Range | Max − Min |
| Q1 / Q3 | First and third quartiles |
| IQR | Interquartile range (Q3 − Q1) |

## Visualizations

- **Box plot**: Shows min, Q1, median, Q3, max for each dataset
- **Histogram**: Frequency distribution with automatic bin sizing

## Comparison Mode

When multiple named datasets are loaded, a summary table renders all statistics side by side for easy comparison.

## Usage

```ts
import { parseInput, addDataset, getAllStats } from "./index.ts";
import { renderDashboard } from "./render.ts";

addDataset("scores", parseInput("72, 85, 90, 78, 95, 88, 76, 92"));
addDataset("grades", parseInput("[65, 70, 80, 85, 90, 95, 100]"));

const html = renderDashboard(getAllStats());
```

- **Histogram**: frequency distribution chart with automatic binning

---
name: playlist-manager
description: Music playlist manager with playback controls, drag-reorder, shuffle, and repeat
metadata:
  contentType: music
  structure: list
  boundary: all
  scale: "2"
---

# Playlist Manager

A music playlist manager that lets users create and manage playlists with full playback controls.

## Features

- **Create playlists** — Name and describe your playlists
- **Add songs** — Include title, artist, album, duration, and album art URL
- **Drag-to-reorder** — Rearrange songs within a playlist via drag and drop
- **Playback controls** — Play, pause, skip forward, skip back with a progress bar
- **Now playing** — Displays current song info with album art
- **Shuffle mode** — Randomize playback order
- **Repeat modes** — Cycle through no-repeat, repeat-all, and repeat-one

## API

### Playlists
- `GET /` — Render the full UI
- `GET /playlists` — List all playlists
- `POST /playlists` — Create a new playlist `{ name, description? }`
- `GET /playlists/:id` — Get a single playlist
- `PUT /playlists/:id` — Update playlist metadata
- `DELETE /playlists/:id` — Delete a playlist

### Songs
- `POST /playlists/:id/songs` — Add a song `{ title, artist, album?, duration, albumArtUrl? }`
- `DELETE /playlists/:id/songs/:songId` — Remove a song
- `PUT /playlists/:id/reorder` — Reorder songs `{ songIds: string[] }`

### Player
- `POST /player/play` — Play `{ playlistId, songIndex? }`
- `POST /player/pause` — Pause
- `POST /player/skip` — Skip to next
- `POST /player/previous` — Go to previous
- `POST /player/shuffle` — Toggle shuffle
- `POST /player/repeat` — Cycle repeat mode
- `GET /player/state` — Get current player state

export type Song = {
  id: string
  title: string
  artist: string
  album: string
  duration: number // seconds
  albumArtUrl: string
}

export type Playlist = {

---
name: playlist-manager
description: Music playlist manager with playback controls, drag-to-reorder, shuffle, and repeat
metadata:
  contentType: music
  structure: list
  boundary: all
  scale: "2"
---

# Playlist Manager

A full-featured music playlist manager that lets users organize and play their music collections.

## Features

- **Create & manage playlists** — name, describe, and organize multiple playlists
- **Song management** — add songs with title, artist, album, duration, and album art URL
- **Drag-to-reorder** — reorder songs within a playlist via drag-and-drop
- **Playback controls** — play, pause, skip forward, skip back with a visual progress bar
- **Now playing** — displays current song info with album art
- **Shuffle mode** — randomize playback order
- **Repeat modes** — repeat the entire playlist or a single track

## Usage

### Create a playlist
POST to `/api/playlists` with a JSON body containing `name` and optional `description`.

### Add a song
POST to `/api/playlists/:id/songs` with `title`, `artist`, `album`, `duration` (seconds), and `albumArtUrl`.

### Reorder songs
PATCH to `/api/playlists/:id/reorder` with `fromIndex` and `toIndex`.

### Playback
- POST `/api/playback/play` — start or resume playback
- POST `/api/playback/pause` — pause playback
- POST `/api/playback/next` — skip to next song
- POST `/api/playback/prev` — skip to previous song
- POST `/api/playback/shuffle` — toggle shuffle mode
- POST `/api/playback/repeat` — cycle repeat mode (none → all → one)

### View
GET `/` renders the full playlist manager UI with now-playing bar and all playlists.

export type Song = {
  id: string
  title: string
  artist: string
  album: string
  duration: number
  albumArtUrl: string
}

export type Playlist = {
  id: string
  name: string
  description: string
  songs: Song[]
  createdAt: string
  updatedAt: string
}

export type RepeatMode = 'none' | 'all' | 'one'

export type PlaybackState = {
  playlistId: string | null
  songIndex: number
  isPlaying: boolean
  shuffle: boolean
  repeat: RepeatMode
  progress: number
  shuffleOrder: number[]
}

export type Store = {
  playlists: Playlist[]
  playback: PlaybackState
}

---
name: playlist-manager
description: Music playlist manager with playback controls, drag-to-reorder, shuffle, and repeat
metadata:
  contentType: music
  structure: list
  boundary: all
  scale: "2"
---

# Playlist Manager

Manage music playlists with full playback controls.

## Features

- **Create playlists** — name and describe your playlists
- **Add songs** — title, artist, album, duration, and album art URL
- **Drag-to-reorder** — rearrange songs within a playlist via drag and drop
- **Playback controls** — play, pause, skip forward, skip back with a progress bar
- **Now playing** — displays current song info with album art
- **Shuffle mode** — randomize playback order
- **Repeat modes** — repeat all, repeat one, or no repeat

## API

- `GET /` — list all playlists
- `GET /playlist/:id` — view a playlist with its songs
- `POST /playlist` — create a new playlist (name, description)
- `POST /playlist/:id/song` — add a song to a playlist
- `PUT /playlist/:id/reorder` — reorder songs (fromIndex, toIndex)
- `DELETE /playlist/:id/song/:songId` — remove a song
- `DELETE /playlist/:id` — delete a playlist
- `POST /playback/play` — play a song (playlistId, songIndex)
- `POST /playback/pause` — pause playback
- `POST /playback/skip` — skip to next song
- `POST /playback/previous` — go to previous song
- `POST /playback/shuffle` — toggle shuffle mode
- `POST /playback/repeat` — cycle repeat mode (none → all → one)
- `POST /playback/seek` — seek to position (progress: 0-100)

export type Song = {
  id: string
  title: string
  artist: string
  album: string
  duration: number
  albumArtUrl: string
}

export type Playlist = {
  id: string
  name: string
  description: string
  songs: Song[]
  createdAt: string
  updatedAt: string
}

export type RepeatMode = 'none' | 'all' | 'one'

export type PlaybackState = {
  playlistId: string | null
  songIndex: number
  isPlaying: boolean
  shuffle: boolean
  repeat: RepeatMode
  progress: number
  shuffleOrder: number[]
}

export type Store = {
  playlists: Playlist[]
  playback: PlaybackState
}

input.content:
---
name: physics-simulator
description: Interactive Newton's laws simulator with pendulum, projectile motion, and spring-mass systems
metadata:
  contentType: simulation
  structure: steps
  boundary: all
  scale: "3"
---

# Physics Simulator

An interactive physics simulator demonstrating Newton's laws through three classic systems:

## Simulations

### 1. Simple Pendulum
- Simulates a pendulum oscillating under gravity
- Adjustable parameters: pendulum length (0.5–3m), gravity (1–20 m/s²)
- Displays angular position, angular velocity, and angular acceleration in real time

### 2. Projectile Motion
- Simulates projectile trajectory under uniform gravity
- Adjustable parameters: launch angle (0–90°), initial velocity (1–50 m/s)
- Shows full trajectory path with real-time position, velocity, and acceleration

### 3. Spring-Mass System
- Simulates a mass on a horizontal spring (Hooke's law)
- Adjustable parameters: spring constant k (1–100 N/m), mass (0.1–10 kg)
- Displays displacement, velocity, and acceleration in real time

## Controls
- **Play/Pause** — start or pause the simulation
- **Reset** — return to initial conditions
- **Speed** — adjust simulation speed (0.25×–4×)

## Usage
Render the module UI and interact with sliders to change parameters. The canvas updates in real time showing the physics and computed values.

---
name: physics-simulator
description: Interactive Newton's laws physics simulator with pendulum, projectile motion, and spring-mass system
metadata:
  contentType: simulation
  structure: object
  boundary: all
  scale: "2"
---

# Physics Simulator

An interactive physics simulator demonstrating Newton's laws of motion through three classic systems.

## Simulations

### 1. Simple Pendulum
Demonstrates oscillatory motion under gravity. The restoring force is proportional to sin(θ), governed by Newton's 2nd Law (F = ma).

**Adjustable parameters:** pendulum length (0.5–5 m), gravity (1–20 m/s²), initial angle (5–90°)

### 2. Projectile Motion
Shows parabolic trajectory under constant gravitational acceleration. Newton's 1st Law governs constant horizontal velocity; the 2nd Law governs vertical acceleration.

**Adjustable parameters:** launch angle (5–85°), initial velocity (5–50 m/s), gravity (1–20 m/s²)

### 3. Spring-Mass System
Demonstrates simple harmonic motion via Hooke's Law (F = -kx) combined with Newton's 2nd and 3rd Laws.

**Adjustable parameters:** spring constant (1–50 N/m), mass (0.1–10 kg), initial displacement (0.1–3 m)

## Features

- Real-time display of position, velocity, and acceleration
- Play / Pause / Reset controls
- Adjustable simulation speed (0.1x–3x)
- Canvas-based rendering with motion trails and force vectors
- Symplectic Euler integration for energy-conserving physics

## Usage

Start the server with `bun run src/index.ts`, then open `http://localhost:3000` in a browser.

---
name: physics-simulator
description: Interactive Newton's laws simulator with pendulum, projectile, and spring-mass simulations
metadata:
  contentType: simulation
  structure: object
  boundary: all
  scale: "2"
---

# Physics Simulator

Interactive simulator demonstrating Newton's laws of motion through three classic physics systems.

## Simulations

### 1. Simple Pendulum
Demonstrates oscillatory motion under gravity. Adjustable parameters:
- **Length** (0.5–3.0 m): Changes the pendulum arm length, affecting period
- **Gravity** (1–20 m/s²): Adjust gravitational acceleration

Displays real-time angular position (θ), angular velocity (ω), and tangential acceleration.

### 2. Projectile Motion
Simulates a launched projectile under uniform gravity. Adjustable parameters:
- **Launch Angle** (5–85°): Initial trajectory angle
- **Initial Velocity** (5–50 m/s): Launch speed

Traces the full parabolic trajectory path and displays position (x, y), velocity (vx, vy), and acceleration.

### 3. Spring-Mass System
Models simple harmonic motion of a mass on a horizontal spring. Adjustable parameters:
- **Spring Constant k** (1–50 N/m): Stiffness of the spring
- **Mass** (0.5–10 kg): Mass of the attached object

Displays displacement from equilibrium, velocity, and acceleration in real time.

## Controls
- **Play/Pause**: Start or pause the simulation
- **Reset**: Return to initial conditions
- **Speed**: Adjust simulation speed from 0.25x to 4x

## Usage
Start the server and open the browser to interact with all three simulations via tabbed navigation.

import { createServer } from './server'

export { createServer } from './server'
export { renderPage } from './ui'
export {
  initPendulum,
  stepPendulum,
  initProjectile,
  stepProjectile,
  initSpringMass,
  stepSpringMass,
} from './physics'
export type {
  PendulumParams,
  PendulumState,
  ProjectileParams,
  ProjectileState,
  SpringMassParams,
  SpringMassState,
  SimulationType,
  Vec2,
} from './types'

const port = parseInt(process.env.PORT ?? '3000', 10)
createServer({ port })
